question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's a comprehensive breakdown of the `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm, along with a complete example.

---

## Required Fields

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `type` | Must be `charm` |
| `name` | Charm name (determines `.charm` filename and Charmhub URL) |
| `summary` | One-line description (max 78 characters) |
| `description` | Extended multi-line description |
| `base` | Target OS, e.g. `ubuntu@24.04` |
| `platforms` | Target architectures to build for |
| `containers` | **(Required for K8s charms)** Sidecar containers deployed with the charm |
| `resources` | OCI images for each container |

---

## Complete Example

```yaml
# === Identity ===
name: my-flask-app
type: charm
title: My Flask App
summary: A Flask web application charm for Kubernetes
description: |
  This charm deploys and manages a Flask web application
  on a Kubernetes cloud using Juju. It supports PostgreSQL
  integration and configurable greetings.

# === Base & Platforms ===
base: ubuntu@24.04
platforms:
  amd64:        # shorthand when build-on == build-for
  arm64:
    build-on: [arm64]
    build-for: [arm64]

# === Build Configuration ===
parts:
  my-charm:
    plugin: charm
    source: .
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt

# === Charm Libraries ===
charm-libs:
  - lib: canonical.data-platform-libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0"

# === Runtime ===
charm-user: non-root

# === Kubernetes Containers & Resources ===
containers:
  flask-app:
    resource: flask-app-image
    mounts:
      - storage: app-data
        location: /app/data

resources:
  flask-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the Flask application

# === Storage ===
storage:
  app-data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent storage for application data
    location: /app/data
    minimum-size: 1G

# === Relations (Integration Endpoints) ===
requires:
  postgresql:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: false
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1

provides:
  metrics-endpoint:
    interface: prometheus_scrape
  grafana-dashboard:
    interface: grafana_dashboard

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my_flask_app_replica

# === Configuration ===
config:
  options:
    greeting:
      type: string
      default: "Hello, world!"
      description: |
        The greeting to be returned by the Flask application.
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: "info"
      description: |
        Logging level for the application. One of: debug, info, warning, error.
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: The port the application listens on.

# === Actions ===
actions:
  rotate-credentials:
    description: Rotate the application's internal credentials.
    params:
      force:
        type: boolean
        default: false
        description: Force credential rotation even if not expired.

# === Feature Requirements ===
assumes:
  - k8s-api
  - any-of:
      - juju >= 3.5
      - juju >= 2.9.23

# === Metadata Links ===
links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-flask-app-docs
  issues: https://github.com/my-org/my-flask-app/issues
  source: https://github.com/my-org/my-flask-app
  contact: maintainers@example.com
  website:
    - https://example.com

# === Linting ===
analysis:
  ignore:
    attributes:
      - check-id
    linters:
      - entrypoint
```

---

## Key Notes for Kubernetes Charms

1. **`containers` is required** — every K8s charm must declare at least one container (sidecar). Each container references either a `resource` (OCI image) or a `bases` list.

2. **`resources` with `type: oci-image`** — each container needs a corresponding OCI image resource. At deploy time, Juju pulls this image for the sidecar.

3. **`assumes: [k8s-api]`** — tells Juju this charm requires a Kubernetes cloud. This prevents accidental deployment on machine clouds.

4. **`charm-user`** — recommended for K8s charms (Juju 3.6+). Options: `root`, `sudoer`, `non-root`.

5. **`platforms`** replaces the deprecated `bases` key. The shorthand form (just the architecture name with no sub-keys) means `build-on` and `build-for` are the same architecture.

6. **`parts`** — controls the build. The `charm` plugin handles Python-based charms; `charm-entrypoint` defaults to `src/charm.py` and `charm-requirements` defaults to `[requirements.txt]`, so these can often be omitted.
